title: Two-Band Hamiltonian A Plus B Dot Sigma
summary: A generic local two-band Hamiltonian can be written as H(p)=a(p)+b(p).sigma, and after subtracting the scalar part the crossing structure is governed by the vector b(p).
aliases: a plus b dot sigma form
assumptions: The problem has been reduced to an effective 2x2 Hermitian Hamiltonian.
regime: Local description near a two-band crossing.
scope: Lecture One universal local form for an isolated crossing.

source_anchors.notes: The vector b(p) controls both the crossing and its topology.

representation: Pauli-matrix decomposition of a local 2x2 Bloch Hamiltonian.
failure_modes: For more than two relevant bands the local reduction may need a larger matrix block.
retrieval_hints: Use for any query about local effective Hamiltonians near Weyl nodes.
